headline: Sabah Hepatitis B Rate 4 Times National Average
context: Sabah Health Department reported hepatitis B prevalence of 8.2% among adults in 2025 four times the national average of 2.1%.

cards.big: Sabah hepatitis B rate at 8.2% is 4 times the 2.1% national average
cards.sub: Approximately 280,000 Sabahans carry the virus with many undiagnosed

cards.big: Only 45% of rural Sabah children received full hepatitis B vaccination by age 2
cards.sub: Urban vaccination rate of 88% matches national but rural clinics lack supply

cards.big: Free screening programme tested only 52,000 of targeted 500,000 adults
cards.sub: Mobile clinic units cover 12 districts but visit rural stations only monthly

cards.big: Liver cancer rates in Sabah 2.8 times higher than Peninsula linked to hepatitis
cards.sub: QEH performed 180 liver-related surgeries in 2025 up from 120 in 2022

cards.big: Health disparity tests whether disease prevention reaches every Malaysian equally
Hepatitis B at 4 times the national rate — Sabah's health crisis requires targeted intervention, not averages.
